briefings.2026-06-30: Several new climbing gym openings are in the pipeline across continental Europe today. In France, father-son duo Bloc'Up has launched an innovative new climbing gym concept in Roquevaire. In Austria, Vienna's Brigittenau district welcomes 'das flash', a new community-focused climbing gym in a converted factory hall. Meanwhile in Germany, the Kaufbeuren sports association has voted to approve construction and operation of a new climbing hall as part of a planned facility expansion.

stories.title: Bloc'Up Aims to Reinvent the Climbing Gym Concept in Roquevaire
stories.summary: Bloc'Up, a new climbing gym in Roquevaire, France, opened on 12 June, launched by a father-son duo offering an innovative take on the climbing gym format. The gym presents a unique concept aimed at differentiating itself in the local climbing market.

stories.title: Kaufbeuren Members' Assembly Approves Construction and Operation of New Climbing Hall
stories.summary: A general members' meeting in Kaufbeuren, Germany has voted to approve the construction and operation of a new climbing hall as part of a planned facility expansion. Chairman Ralf Trinkwalder presented the concept for the expansion at the assembly.

stories.title: 'Das Flash' - New Community Climbing Gym Opens in Vienna's Brigittenau District
stories.summary: A new community-oriented climbing gym called 'das flash' has opened in a converted factory hall in Vienna's Brigittenau district. The venue aims to bring new life and activity to the local neighbourhood.
